sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: 12 months of operations, maintenance, and improvements for the Eternl non-custodial Cardano light wallet, including frontend and backend development, infrastructure, support, administration, and fund-use audits.

Deliverables: Frontend maintenance and cross-platform development for web, browser extension, iOS, Android, and beta channels, Backend infrastructure and operations including multi-region servers, Cardano nodes, DBSync, application services, metadata aggregation, monitoring, and incident response, User support through Discord ticketing, Telegram, public channels, project support, wiki maintenance, and educational videos, Governance tooling enhancements including DRep dashboards, proposal browsers, in-wallet voting, and proposal creation, Eternl Core rewrite, Eternl Hub, enhanced hardware wallet support, enhanced wallet data export, and ongoing operations, Periodic independent audits of treasury fund use and oversight metrics
Deadline/expiry: 12-month delivery period from August 2026 to July 2027
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Eternl says it is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet available on web, browser extension, Android, and iOS.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳2,350,000 at about $0.1787 per Ada to cover an annual operating cost of about $420,000.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The budget allocates 55% to frontend, 25% to backend, 5% to support, 13% to admin, and 2% to audits.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl says 10 people contribute to the project and this proposal funds 6.0 FTE at $70,000 per FTE.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl plans to introduce paid Pro plans at $96 per year for personal users and $360 per year for companies, and says about 5,500 Pro users would fully cover the $420,000 annual cost.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl says it has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 installs across Android and iOS.
missing: Independent evidence for: Eternl says it is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet available on web, browser extension, Android, and iOS.
Independent evidence for: Eternl says it has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 installs across Android and iOS.

Independent evidence for: Eternl says 10-18% of Cardano mainnet transactions are conducted via Eternl and that it has provided access across several platforms with basically zero downtime for over five years.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests ₳2,350,000 at about $0.1787 per Ada to cover an annual operating cost of about $420,000." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Eternl says it is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet available on web, browser extension, Android, and iOS." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
